expected_trades_per_day_basis: Estimated ~340 filtered events over 30d window (synthesizer note) ≈ 11.3 trades/day; but US-session gating (8h of 24h) plus taker_buy>=0.60 plus high-liquidity bucket likely compresses to 3-5/day in practice — using midpoint of declared sample.

best_in: US equity-market open hours (13:30-20:00 UTC) with rising BTC trend
high-liquidity majors (BTC/ETH/SOL/top-20) during NY session overlap
positive funding regime where taker-buy aggression aligns with spot bid
worst_in: Asia-only or low-volume weekend sessions (filter excludes by design but edge cases of DST/holiday US closures)
BTC range-bound chop with taker_buy print noise (false-positive aggression)
macro-event days (CPI/FOMC) where 5m taker_buy spikes are reactive, not directional
regime_sensitivity.explanation: The strategy explicitly conditions on US session liquidity + sustained taker-buy aggression on a pricechange long signal, so it harvests momentum continuation when NY desks are active and majors have tight spreads. It degrades when taker-buy ratio is noisy from thin books or when macro prints cause whipsaw aggression that reverts within the holding window.

live_readiness_caveats: Restrict to top-20 USDT-perp symbols only — high-liquidity bucket must be enforced live by a hard symbol whitelist or rolling-volume filter, otherwise mid-cap fills will slip and erase the session edge

Devil flag is real: 30d window contains ~22 US sessions, so a single macro-event cluster (e.g. one FOMC week) can dominate the 340-event sample — require sub-window stability check (split into 3x10d) before promoting beyond paper
Session boundary handling: entries fired in the last 15min of US session may hold past close into Asia thin-book — cap entry cutoff at session_end - holding_period or skip late-session triggers

monitoring_metrics.rationale: Frequency drift signals filter degradation — taker_buy_ratio_5m threshold may be too loose/tight in live data vs backtest snapshot

monitoring_metrics.alert_threshold: close-third median flips negative for >5 sessions
monitoring_metrics.rationale: Distinguishes genuine session edge from open-only momentum; close-third decay is the most likely failure mode given Devil's macro-overfit flag
Pump+taker_buy entries chase momentum; high slippage directly eats the +0.43 lift this archetype claims vs median
E126 paper-shadow precedent: high backtest scores compounded to -13.5%; consecutive-loss runs are the practical signature of left-tail risk
If the edge collapses to 'long when BTC is up', the session/taker_buy/liquidity stack is decorative — kill and replace with a simpler BTC-trend filter

stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Matches the v2 metric framework that flagged E126's asymmetric TP/SL trap; kelly_capped is the canonical live-readiness gate per R2 lessons

stop_trading_triggers.condition: Sub-window split (last 10d vs prior 20d) shows median trade_pct sign-flip
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Directly addresses Devil's macro-overfit concern — non-stationary edge is the dominant failure mode for session-gated strategies

stop_trading_triggers.condition: 3 consecutive US sessions with zero qualifying triggers
stop_trading_triggers.rationale: Indicates the taker_buy_ratio_5m>=0.60 + high_liquidity intersection has shifted out of distribution; either market regime changed or data pipeline broke — investigate before continuing

expected_max_dd_basis: Long-only pricechange-channel entries historically run 8-12% paper DD in 30d windows (cf. E126's -13.5% compounded path); session gating should reduce variance modestly but high-volatility US opens can cluster losses, so 9.5% is a midpoint estimate at 5-10% sizing.
compare_to_existing: Closest sibling is E051 pc_high_liquidity_long (best=+0.347, trig=2157) — same channel/side/liquidity bucket but unsessioned; E212 should outperform E051 during US hours and underperform outside them. Triangulate by running both in paper-shadow concurrently and comparing US-session-only return distributions.
